Please note we are looking to speak to individuals already based in the Riyadh/Dubai as a first priority.

Capco, a Wipro company, is a global technology and management consultancy specializing in driving digital transformation in the financial services industry. With a growing client portfolio comprising of over 100 global organizations, Capco operates at the intersection of business and technology by combining innovative thinking with unrivalled industry knowledge to deliver end-to-end data-driven solutions and fast-track digital initiatives for banking and payments, capital markets, wealth and asset management, insurance, and the energy sector. Capco’s cutting-edge ingenuity is brought to life through its Innovation Labs and award-winning Be Yourself At Work culture and diverse talent.

Location: Riyadh/Dubai (On-site) | Practice Area: Data & AI | Type: Permanent

The Role

As a Managing Principal / Director – Data Lead, you’ll play a pivotal role in growing Capco’s Data & Analytics capability in Riyadh. This is a senior, hands-on leadership position that blends delivery ownership with market development. You’ll lead high-impact data programmes for major clients while actively contributing to sales activity, account growth, and the expansion of Capco’s footprint in the region.

What You’ll Do

Lead end-to-end delivery of complex data and analytics engagements, remaining hands-on enough to guide technical direction and outcomesAct as a trusted senior advisor to client stakeholders, translating business objectives into scalable data solutionsDrive business development activity, supporting proposals, shaping client opportunities, and building long-term client relationshipsBuild and grow Capco’s Data & Analytics presence in Riyadh, contributing to capability development and local market strategyMentor and develop teams, fostering an inclusive, high-performing environment aligned to Capco values

What We’re Looking For

Experience in leading Consulting firms10+ years’ practical experience in data, analytics or AI roles with significant, demonstrable exposure to the financial services industrySenior leadership experience delivering large-scale data and analytics programmes within consulting or complex client environmentsStrong technical grounding across data platforms, architecture, and analytics, with the ability to engage credibly in delivery discussionsExcellent stakeholder management skills, with confidence working at executive levelDemonstrable consulting background and experience in delivery or sales, account growth and client development.

Bonus Points For

Experience working in or across Middle Eastern marketsBackground in financial services or adjacent regulated industriesExperience shaping new market offerings or growing regional practicesNative Arabic speaker

Why Join Capco

Deliver high-impact data and analytics solutions for leading organisationsPlay a key role in growing Capco’s Data & AI in the Middle EastWork in a collaborative, flat, and entrepreneurial consulting culture

Inclusion at Capco

We’re committed to making our recruitment process accessible and straightforward for everyone. If you need any adjustments at any stage, just let us know – we’ll be happy to help. We value each person’s unique perspective and contribution. At Capco, we believe that being yourself is your greatest strength. Our #BeYourselfAtWork culture encourages individuality and collaboration – a mindset that shapes how we work with clients and each other every day.

review.title: Head of Business Development
review.jd_text:
About Us:KPay Group (“KPay”) is a leading fintech company committed to empowering businesses of all sizes through simple, smart, seamless, and secure technology solutions. Serving over 72,000 merchants across Australia, Hong Kong, Japan, and Singapore, KPay helps unlock growth potential by providing a one-stop platform for financial management, business operations, and digital transformation. In 2024, KPay raised a record-breaking USD 55 million in its Series A funding round—the largest globally in the payments sector that year. The company has since been recognised as PayTech of the Year at the Asia FinTech Awards and named to Forbes Asia’s 100 to Watch list in 2025.
About the Role:As the first business leader on the ground in the UAE, you will architect and execute our go-to-market strategy, build and lead the early BD/sales team, and establish KPay as a trusted partner for UAE merchants. This is a true zero-to-one role: you will shape our market entry, define our sales playbook, cultivate key partnerships, and directly drive early revenue—laying the foundation for scalable growth. If you thrive in building from scratch, excel in high-ambiguous environments, and are motivated by creating something impactful, this is your opportunity.
Job Description:Build & own the UAE GTM strategy – develop and execute the market entry plan, including target segments, pricing approach, partnership roadmap, and sales model tailored to the UAE ecosystem.Lead from the front – personally drive early enterprise and key merchant deals while building, mentoring, and scaling a high-performing BD team.Design & implement sales operations – establish CRM processes, pipeline governance, performance metrics, and incentive frameworks to ensure transparency, velocity, and predictability.Forge strategic partnerships – identify, negotiate, and manage alliances with banks, fintechs, ISVs, and channel partners to accelerate market penetration.Own the revenue number – achieve and exceed quarterly targets for merchant acquisition, transaction volume, and revenue growth.
Job Requirements:12+ years in business development, sales, or GTM roles, with a proven track record of building and scaling revenue in early-stage or high-growth B2B SME environments (fintech, payments, SaaS preferred).Deep understanding of the UAE business landscape – existing network, cultural fluency, and knowledge of local regulations, competitive dynamics, and customer behavior.A builder’s mindset – experience designing sales processes, team structures, and partnership frameworks from scratch, not just managing established pipelines.Strong leadership presence – ability to inspire, coach, and grow a team while remaining hands-on in key deals and strategic conversations.Excellent communication and relationship skills – persuasive in both one-on-one and group settings, able to engage C-level stakeholders and partners with credibility.Resilient and entrepreneurial – thrives in ambiguity, learns quickly, and operates with urgency, integrity, and a merchant-first attitude.

review.title: Head of Compliance, EMEA
review.jd_text:
Who We Are:

At OKX, we believe that the future will be reshaped by Crypto, ultimately contributing to every individual's freedom. OKX began as a crypto exchange giving millions of people access to crypto trading and over time becoming among the largest platforms in the world. In recent years, we have developed one of the most connected Web3 wallets used by millions to access decentralized crypto applications (dApps). OKX is a trusted brand by hundreds of large institutions seeking access to crypto markets on a reliable platform that seamlessly connects with global banking and payments.

We are deeply committed to shaping a fairer, more transparent and accessible society through blockchain technology. This is why we publish proof of reserves monthly, and continue to ship new innovative security features.

About The Team:

The Compliance function at OKX is evolving. We are not just a control function; we are a strategic partner to the business. We are a team of risk-minded problem solvers and operational executors who ensure the business runs safely at speed. We advise on regulatory obligations while relentlessly optimizing our processes to allow OKX to scale efficiently.

What You'll Be Doing:

As the Head of Compliance, Europe, you will be the operational engine behind our European compliance strategy.You will move beyond high-level advisory to become a true executor, responsible for bringing the compliance team to its next level of maturity.

Your mandate is clear: make OKX's European compliance run faster and better. You will lead a diverse team of country heads and MLROs, transforming them into a cohesive, high-performance unit. You will drive operational excellence, reduce friction in our controls, and support strong compliance controls without slowing down commercial growth.

What You'll Be Doing:

Drive the strategic direction and the execution of the Compliance program across all OKX entities within Europe. Manage and elevate the performance of Country Heads of Compliance and MLROs, moving the team to a more unified, fast-moving regional force. Audit current workflows and ruthlessly eliminate inefficiencies. Implement robust, scalable frameworks that reduce manual workload and utilize data/tech to speed up decision-making. Liaise with first, second, and third-line teams to build a control environment that exceeds regulatory standards while maintaining commercial agility. Oversee compliance reviews and investigations. Produce data-driven compliance reports for management and the Board. You will focus on metrics, SLAs, and KPIs that demonstrate the speed and effectiveness of the compliance function. Participate in Board committee and sub-committee meetings, whether as a member or on an as-needed basis. Act as the primary escalation point for complex issues, providing decisive, commercially aware guidance to business stakeholders. 


What We Look For In You:

8+ years of experience in compliance within financial services, FinTech or cryptocurrency industries, with a specific track record of operationalizing compliance strategies. You don't just identify problems, you fix them. You have a proven ability to work fast, meet tight deadlines, and drive projects to completion in a high-pressure environment. Experience reporting into or working closely with Operations teams. You understand how to balance regulatory safety with operational speed. Deep understanding of EU regulations (MiCA, GDPR, AMLD5/6) and UK regimes. You know how to navigate the fragmented European regulatory landscape. Strong people management skills with experience leading dispersed teams. You know how to rally a team around a common goal and drive performance. You are comfortable using data to measure team performance, identify bottlenecks, and justify resource allocation. Crisp, high-impact communication skills. You can explain complex regulatory concepts to product teams and operational challenges to regulators. 


Nice To Haves:

Direct experience scaling a crypto or high-growth fintech company in Europe. Prior experience working in the crypto industry. CAMS, ICA, or equivalent certification. 


Perks & Benefits

Competitive total compensation package L&D programs and Education subsidy for employees' growth and development Various team building programs and company events Wellness and meal allowances Comprehensive healthcare schemes for employees and dependants More that we love to tell you along the process! 


Notice:

All official OKX vacancies are published on this website. While roles may appear on selected third-party platforms from time to time, information on other sites may be inaccurate or outdated. If in doubt, please apply directly through our official careers website.

Information collected and processed as part of the recruitment process of any job application you choose to submit is subject to OKX's Candidate Privacy Notice.

review.title: Director Transaction Advisory
review.jd_text:
Here at Buro Happold, expect the exceptional

From the projects you will work on to the clients we partner with. From career‑defining opportunities to an inclusive and diverse practice where everyone contributes to a more equitable world. From our reputation to our relationships. Wherever your Buro Happold career takes you, it will be anything but ordinary.

Here at Buro Happold, expect the exceptional. 

From the projects you will work on to the clients we work with. From career-defining opportunities to an inclusive and diverse practice where we all contribute to a more equitable world. From our reputation to our relationships. Wherever your Buro Happold career takes you, it’ll be anything but ordinary.

Your next role

Buro Happold is supporting the transformation of cities and public services across the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the wider GCC, aligned with Vision 2030, national transformation programmes, and public‑sector reform agendas. As Director – Transaction Advisory within our Municipal Services team, you will play a pivotal leadership role in shaping, structuring, and delivering complex public‑sector transactions across the region.

Based in KSA, you will lead Transaction Advisory services across priority municipal sectors including urban services, mobility, utilities, public realm, mixed-use development, sport and social infrastructure. You will work closely with senior public‑sector stakeholders and delivery entities.

This role offers a rare opportunity to combine deep KSA market understanding, strong knowledge of the local NCP PPP lifecycle framework, and global best practice to help public authorities structure bankable, resilient, and outcome‑driven transactions that deliver long‑term value for citizens.

Your key duties

As a Director, you will provide senior leadership to our Transaction Advisory and PPP offering within Municipal Services, driving growth, credibility, and excellence in delivery across KSA and the GCC.

You will:

Lead the Transaction Advisory strategy in KSA and the GCC for public‑sector projects, aligned with Buro Happold’s regional growth.Originate and lead PPP, privatization, and transaction advisory engagements, including project screening, feasibility studies, business cases, value‑for‑money assessments, and procurement strategies.Advise public‑sector clients across the full PPP lifecycle, including project identification, structuring, market sounding, procurement, bid evaluation, and support through financial close.Act as a trusted advisor to senior government, municipal, and delivery authority leadership, supporting decisions on service delivery models, private‑sector participation, and long‑term operating arrangements.Provide expert advice on commercial, contractual, and risk‑allocation structures, ensuring transactions are bankable, compliant with KSA frameworks, and aligned with policy and affordability objectives.Work closely with multidisciplinary teams (engineering, planning, operations, digital, economics) to deliver integrated, technically robust, and commercially sound transaction solutions.Lead high‑level client engagements, workshops, negotiations, and market‑facing interactions with government entities, investors, developers, operators, and lenders.Drive business development across KSA and the GCC, including strategic client relationships, opportunity origination, bid leadership, and framework participation.Represent Buro Happold with NCP, municipalities, ministries, and key industry stakeholders, strengthening our position as a trusted advisor in PPP and public‑sector transactions.Mentor and develop regional advisory talent, building a strong, locally grounded Transaction Advisory capability within the Municipal Services team.Oversee portfolio performance, ensuring quality assurance, commercial success, and effective risk management across advisory commissions.


Your Skills And Experience

You will bring a combination of regional credibility, transaction expertise, and senior‑level leadership experience.

You will have:

A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Engineering, or a related discipline; a postgraduate qualification is highly desirable.Significant experience in Transaction Advisory, PPP, or Commercial Advisory, gained within a leading consultancy, advisory firm, or financial institution.Strong, demonstrable experience in the KSA market, including a clear understanding of government structures, procurement regulations, and the NCP PPP process/Framework.A proven track record delivering PPP, privatization, or investment transactions across municipal, infrastructure, or public‑service sectors in KSA and/or the GCC.Deep understanding of project delivery models, operating models, funding and financing structures, and risk allocation in public‑sector transactions.Established relationships with government entities, NCP, municipalities, investors, developers, and operators in the region.Strong leadership and people‑development skills,

review.jd_text:
Join Us At Miral Experiences

Miral Experiences is a region’s leader in the management and operation of world-class, award-winning immersive attractions and experiences across Abu Dhabi. Our diverse portfolio includes some of the most iconic entertainment and leisure destinations, including Ferrari World Yas Island Abu Dhabi, Yas Waterworld Yas Island Abu Dhabi, Warner Bros. World™ Yas Island Abu Dhabi, SeaWorld Yas Island Abu Dhabi, CLYMB™ Yas Island Abu Dhabi, and TeamLab. Phenomena Abu Dhabi, and CLYMB™ Abu Dhabi, as well as cultural treasures like Qasr Al Watan. We specialize in crafting unique experiences that cater to all tastes and ages, delivering a seamless blend of thrills, joy, and discovery. Our attractions are designed to ignite the imagination and create unforgettable memories for visitors from around the world. Whether you're seeking excitement, relaxation, or cultural enrichment, we offer something for everyone.

About The Role

As the Facilities Asset Management Director, you will drive the strategic development and execution of facility management policies to ensure the seamless operation and maintenance of all physical assets. You will oversee comprehensive CAPEX and OPEX budgets, direct major upgrades and modifications, and guarantee compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations. Leading the Hard and Soft Services Divisions—including HVAC, plumbing, irrigation, and civil works—you will analyze equipment failures, optimize cost-effective practices, and manage a high-performing facilities team. Your role demands a blend of technical expertise, leadership, and operational acumen to maintain peak efficiency, minimize downtime, and uphold the highest standards of safety and performance across our parks.

What You'll Do

Facility Operations & Maintenance

Supervise and manage all facilities operations, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and operational excellence.Plan, direct, and control all aspects of leisure facility maintenance to maintain optimal functionality and guest satisfaction.

Manage Division Profit & Loss (P&L), collaborating with Finance and Senior Leadership to track expenditures against budgets.

Team Leadership & Development

Contractor & Vendor Management

Safety & Regulatory Compliance

Prioritize the safety of colleagues and guests by ensuring adherence to government health and environmental regulations (e.g., HACCP, firefighting systems).Address and rectify items listed in the Safety Log from HSE in a timely manner.Ensure all colleagues are properly trained and utilize safety equipment to reduce workplace injuries.

review.title: Head of Asset Management & AI Transformation
review.jd_text:
Remote | Global Role | Multi-Industry

This is not a traditional asset management role

This role is designed for someone who understands that the future of maintenance and asset management is driven by data, reliability engineering, and intelligent systems.

If your experience is rooted in Reliability-Centred Maintenance (RCM), preventive and predictive strategies, labour optimisation, and cost control, and you are interested in applying this knowledge to AI-driven solutions, this is a unique opportunity to operate at the forefront of the industry.

About MaxAccelerate Technology Group

MaxAccelerate Technology Group is a global technology and transformation business focused on helping asset-intensive organisations improve performance, reduce cost, and scale operations through data, systems, and AI.

Through our ecosystem, including DataMax AI, we work with organisations across Facilities Management, Transportation, Pharma, Manufacturing, Energy, and Infrastructure to transform how assets are managed.

Our approach is grounded in real operational understanding:

 Maintenance strategies must be structured, measurable, and optimised  Systems such as IBM Maximo and other EAM platforms must deliver actionable insight  AI must be applied in a way that drives tangible outcomes across cost, labour, and asset performance 

The Role

We are seeking a Head of Asset Management & AI Transformation to lead the definition and delivery of AI-driven asset management solutions.

The focus of the role is to take deep operational knowledge — including:

 RCM frameworks  Preventive and condition-based maintenance strategies  Workforce planning and labour models  Spare parts, inventory, and asset lifecycle considerations 

and translate these into structured AI use cases and scalable solutions.

Key Responsibilities

 Work closely with Heads of Maintenance, Engineering Leaders, and Operations teams to understand:  Maintenance strategies and maturity  Preventive vs reactive ratios  Labour utilisation and workforce efficiency  Spare parts management and inventory control  Define and structure AI use cases that address:  Reduction in reactive maintenance  Optimisation of preventive maintenance schedules  Labour efficiency and workforce planning  Spare parts forecasting and cost reduction  Asset lifecycle optimisation  Develop and lead Proof of Concepts (PoCs) that validate:  Operational improvements  Cost savings  Performance uplift  Translate business requirements into clear scope of works for DataMax AI R&D teams  Ensure AI solutions integrate effectively with EAM/CMMS platforms (Maximo preferred) and align with real operational workflows  Build clear ROI models, ensuring every solution delivers measurable impact across:  Maintenance costs  Asset uptime  Labour efficiency  Inventory and working capital  Support clients in adopting solutions that improve their infrastructure, operations, and long-term asset strategy 

Why This Role Matters

Most organisations have:

 Maintenance strategies defined on paper  Systems implemented but underutilised  Data available but not fully leveraged 

This role exists to bridge that gap.

You will ensure that:

 Maintenance frameworks such as RCM are not just theoretical, but digitised and optimised  Preventive strategies are continuously refined based on real performance data  Labour and cost structures are aligned with actual operational demand  AI is applied in a way that improves real infrastructure outcomes, not just reporting 

What We Are Looking For

 Strong background in Asset Management, Maintenance, or Engineering leadership  Deep understanding of:  RCM and reliability frameworks  Preventive and predictive maintenance strategies  Workforce and labour optimisation  Spare parts and inventory management  Experience working with EAM/CMMS platforms, ideally IBM Maximo  Ability to translate operational challenges into structured, solution-driven approaches  Commercial awareness and the ability to link technical improvements to cost and performance outcomes 

The Opportunity

This role offers the opportunity to work across industries and influence how organisations manage and optimise critical assets.

You will play a key role in:

 Defining how AI is applied to asset management in real environments  Delivering measurable improvements in cost, performance, and efficiency  Shaping solutions that can be scaled across multiple industries 

Apply

If you are interested in moving beyond traditional asset management roles and want to be involved in shaping the next generation of maintenance and asset optimisation, we would welcome a conversation.
review.verdict_reason: Director-level with AI transformation angle is appealing but domain is reliability-centered asset management (RCM, maintenance engineering) - mismatched with candidate's core DT/PMO/Operations focus despite AI overlay

2 - Project Title

Establishment of and Support to Public Diplomacy Think Tank, Prince Sultan University (PSU), (Project Number: 01004920).

3 - Project Description

Through the partnership between the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) and Prince Sultan University (PSU), this project supports the establishment and operationalisation of the Public Diplomacy Think Tank in Saudi Arabia. The initiative represents Phase II of a multi-phase cooperation, building on the successful completion of Phase I, which defined the strategic foundation and institutional design of the Think Tank.

The Think Tank will strengthen Saudi Arabia’s capacity for public diplomacy by advancing research, innovation, and capacity development aligned with Vision 2030. It will serve as a centre of excellence to promote thought leadership, knowledge exchange, and cross-sector partnerships, positioning PSU as a national and regional leader in public diplomacy.

UNDP provides technical and operational support to ensure the Think Tank’s establishment adheres to international standards, integrates sustainable governance systems, and strengthens PSU’s institutional capacity. PSU will lead in developing research, training, and outreach programmes to enhance Saudi Arabia’s influence, promote global dialogue, and develop local expertise in digital diplomacy and soft power strategies.

The project contributes to the UNSDCF Outcome 4: fostering peaceful, just, and inclusive societies and mobilizing means required for the implementation of Agenda 2030. It aligns with UNDP Strategic Plan Output 3.1 (economic diversification through research, innovation, and digital transformation) and CPD Output 4.3 (strengthening institutional and technical capacities of civil society and academic partners).

review.title: Head of IP Strategy (Intellectual Property), Technology Transfer & Innovation Office
review.company: KAUST (King Abdullah University of Science and Technology)
review.jd_text:
Head of (Intellectual Property) IP Strategy, Technology Transfer & Innovation Office
Research Translation Hub, Technology Transfer & Innovation Office (TTIO) 
Position SummaryThe Head of IP Strategy provides expert leadership in the management and strategic oversight of KAUST’s IP portfolio and team. As a core member of the TTIO Leadership Team, the role contributes to the development and execution of TTIO strategy, drives team performance and development, and cultivates strong internal and external stakeholder relationships to advance the University’s IPobjectives and reputation.
Major Responsibilities
Expert IP ManagementExecute the role of IP Principal, maintaining focus on both the operational and strategic dimensions of the function, and providing expert technical leadership in the management of KAUST’s intellectual property portfolio, commercialization activities, and highly complex IP matters.TTIO LeadershipServe as a core member of the TTIO Leadership Team, actively contributing to meetings and the development and execution of TTIO strategy as directed by the TTIO Director.Oversee the health and performance of the TTIO pipeline, including prioritization, team workload and capability development, budget oversight, risk management, and process optimization.Provide regular reporting, insights, and analysis to inform strategic decision-making.Monitor and advance IP Strategy Team and TTIO KPIs, maintaining accountability for theeffective management and performance of KAUST’s IP portfolio.Contribute to the continuous improvement of TTIO strategy, operating model, efficiency, and effectiveness, and identify/implement innovative opportunities to enhance TTIO initiatives.Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders, including senior leadership, partners, service providers, and attorneys.Develop and maintain a strong external profile that enhances the reputation of TTIO and KAUST, including representation at external meetings, workshops, and conferences.Lead the development and periodic review of KAUST’s institutional IP strategy to ensurealignment with research, commercialization, and national innovation priorities.
Team Talent DevelopmentEngage with IP Strategy Team members to assess skills, capabilities, and development needs.Develop individual and team development plans to address capability gaps, cultivate talent, and support succession planning aligned with TTIO and KAUST business priorities.Conduct regular one-on-one meetings with team members to support professional development.
Team Performance ManagementActively manage and maintain accountability for the performance of the IP Strategy Team and the IP sprint process, including workload, timeliness, quality, responsiveness, and follow-through.Address performance issues promptly to maintain high standards.
Being Part of the TTIODemonstrate and model the values and professional standards of TTIO and KAUST.Perform other related duties as directed by the TTIO Director.
Personal Requirements
CompetenciesExpert understanding of IP laws and practice in Saudi Arabia and the United States.Strong delegation skills to manage workload effectively while maintaining leadership focus and supporting team development.Demonstrated initiative, reliability, and credibility as a senior leader.Understanding of IP laws and practice in jurisdictions beyond Saudi Arabia and the US is desirable.Demonstrates an efficient, effective, and transparent approach to workProactive and responsive in engagement with colleagues and stakeholders.Collaborative leader who inspires confidence and trust.Applies sound, commercially focused judgment and supports data-driven decision-making.Committed to continual improvement and professional excellence.Exceptional verbal and written communication skills; communicate clearly, concisely, respectfully, and professionally.
QualificationsBachelor's degree in law, Science, Engineering, or a related field (advanced degree preferred).Professional certification or qualification as an IP Manager, Specialist, or Professional.
ExperienceA minimum of 12 years of relevant post-qualification experience in intellectual property.Extensive experience advising on and managing a broad range of IP issues and disputes, including inventions, trade secrets, copyright, software, and designs.Demonstrated experience in leading an IP portfolio and advising on IP aspects of commercial transactions.Proven experience in leading and developing an IP team, contributing to IP strategy, and developing organizational IP policies.
review.verdict_reason: Director-level in target geography but IP/tech transfer commercialization is a narrow niche outside candidate's DT/PMO breadth

review.title: Commercial Director, Middle East & North Africa, Cardiometabolic
review.jd_text:
Working at Abbott

At Abbott, you can do work that matters, grow, and learn, care for yourself and your family, be your true self, and live a full life. You’ll also have access to :

Career development with an international company where you can grow the career you dream of.A company recognized as a great place to work in dozens of countries worldwide and named one of the most admired companies in the world by Fortune.A company that is recognized as one of the best big companies to work for as well as the best place to work for diversity, working mothers, female executives, and scientists.

The Opportunity

This position works out of our Dubai location in the Cardiometabolic & Rapid Diagnostics division.

About Rapid Diagnostics Division

Our rapid diagnostic solutions are used in hospitals, laboratories, and clinics around the globe. The crucial information derived from our tests, instruments, and informatics systems is often the first step in patient care decision-making for hundreds of health conditions from heart attacks to blood disorders to infectious diseases and cancers.

The Commercial Director, Middle East and North Africa - CM is accountable for defining and executing the commercial strategy for Abbott Cardiometabolic Diagnostics division across 19 diverse markets within the region. This leadership role is responsible for driving sustainable revenue growth, achieving profitability objectives, and expanding market share across multiple product portfolios.

The incumbent will lead the development and implementation of comprehensive sales strategies, leveraging deep market intelligence and competitive insights to identify growth opportunities and mitigate risks. This position requires a strong ability to translate strategic vision into actionable plans that deliver measurable business outcomes aligned with Abbott’s global objectives

The incumbent leads and mentors a team of Country Leads, Key Account Managers, Distributor partners and technical field personnel, cultivating a high-performance culture focused on flawless commercial execution. The position orchestrates contract negotiations with external customers, ensuring excellence in tendering and contracting processes. Additionally, the Commercial Director fosters an insights-driven culture, informing cross-functional stakeholders of market dynamics and competitive activities to enable strategic decision-making. The role also oversees the implementation of company policies, ensuring operational excellence and compliance across all markets.

What You'll Do-

Commercial Strategy & Execution

Develop and implement regional sales strategies aligned with short- and long-term business objectives.Establish annual sales quotas, forecasts, and pricing strategies, ensuring accurate revenue and profit projections.Drive business growth through acquisition of new customers and expansion of existing accounts.Lead contract negotiations and tendering processes, ensuring competitive and compliant submissions.Monitor market dynamics, competitive activities, and regulatory changes to inform strategic decisions.

Sales Management & Performance Optimization

Plan, direct, and monitor sales activities, providing monthly performance feedback against forecasts.Manage sales administration, customer service, credit collections, invoicing, and ensure timely order processing.Coach Country Leads, Key Account Managers, and field teams on sales techniques, promotions, and execution excellence.Maintain high-level relationships with clients, consultants, and external organizations to strengthen Abbott’s market position.

Leadership & Organizational Development

Foster a diverse and inclusive workplace that empowers all team members to contribute fully toward organizational objectives.Create an entrepreneurial environment, recruiting, coaching, and developing top talent across the region.Keep Abbott’s vision, mission, and values at the forefront of decision-making and daily operations.Demonstrate effective change leadership, ensuring smooth transitions during organizational or market shifts.Build strategic partnerships with internal and external stakeholders to advance departmental and organizational goals.

Operational Excellence & Compliance

Oversee customer service and supply chain operations to ensure efficient product availability and stock flow.Ensure strict compliance with company policies, regulations, and ethical standards.Make operational decisions regarding work processes, resource allocation, and scheduling to achieve departmental objectives.Maintain company standards and procedures across all commercial activities.

Market Development & Insights

Market Development & InsightsGenerate opportunities with existing and new customers, concluding sales calls independently and collaboratively with Marketing.Provide field-based support to commercial partners to drive product sales growth.Assess current and future product opportunities and pursue strategic initiatives to max

The Systems & Service Operations Director for Controls is accountable for operational governance and execution excellence across the Controls Order-to-Cash lifecycle. This role ensures disciplined Percentage of Completion management, predictable backlog-to-invoice conversion, and strong system governance across countries and Controls domains. The position establishes standard work through robust SOPs and provides functional leadership to country and domain systems leaders to ensure consistency, compliance, and cash velocity. The role also defines SOP and governance for pre sales and after sales engineering for EMEA.

Key Responsibilities

How you will do it:

Controls Service Order-to-Cash Governance

Own end-to-end governance of the Controls Order-to-Cash process from order release through execution, revenue recognition, invoicing, and cash realization.Ensure Controls projects are executed in line with contractual milestones, system readiness, and commercial commitments.Act as the single operational authority connecting project execution systems, finance processes, and service delivery.

Percentage of Completion Management

Establish and govern a standardized Percentage of Completion framework for Controls service projects.Ensure accurate progress measurement tied to physical completion, system commissioning, and cost consumption.Drive consistent application of POC rules across countries to eliminate revenue volatility and late recognition.Partner with Finance to ensure compliance with accounting standards and internal controls.

Service Systems Leadership & Country Governance

Provide functional leadership and governance to Country and Domain Controls Service Systems Leaders.Define operating rhythms, review cadences, and escalation paths for system execution risks.Ensure consistent system usage and data quality across Controls service platforms.

Standard Operating Procedures & Standard Work

Design, deploy, and maintain structured SOPs covering all critical Controls service workflows including:Order handoff from Projects to ServiceProgress updates and POC validationChange order and variation handlingInvoicing readiness and releaseDrive adoption of standard work to reduce execution variability across countries and service teams.
Backlog Health & Invoicing Conversion

Monitor Controls service backlog health including aging, stalled projects, and unbilled value.Identify root causes preventing backlog conversion such as system gaps, milestone misalignment, or execution delays.Lead corrective actions to improve invoice timeliness and backlog burn rate.Ensure invoicing reflects true execution status and contractual terms.

Performance Management & Continuous Improvement

Define and track key Controls service KPIs including:POC accuracy and forecast reliabilityBacklog aging and conversion rateInvoice cycle timeRevenue leakage and reworkLead structured problem-solving to address systemic execution issues.Drive continuous improvement using lean, standard work, and operational excellence principles.
Cross-Functional Alignment

Work closely with Controls Service Operations, Project Management, Finance, IT, and Commercial teams.Ensure execution systems support scalable Controls growth and digital service expansion.Align operational processes with customer delivery expectations and long-term service lifecycle objectives.

What We Look For

Senior leadership experience in operations, project operations, or systems governance within a global, multi-country environment.Strong understanding of project execution, commissioning workflows, and service lifecycle management.Deep expertise in Order-to-Cash processes and Percentage of Completion Accounting.Proven ability to design and enforce SOPs and standard work at scale.Strong influencing skills in matrix organizations with indirect leadership accountability.Highly analytical, data-driven, and execution-focused leadership style.
